title: Better variety than Avengers and Spiderman playsets
text:
I recommend this playset for some variety. The Avengers playset and the Spiderman playset are way too similar to each other.

This is a nice change - the playset world is quite different than the standard city scape. The missions are pretty similar (collect things, kill things, rinse and repeat) but its fun getting to use the Guardians of the Galaxy figures in a playset.

My family likes Rocket Raccoon the most - so I recommend picking him up if you get this playset.

title: Decent case
text:
Wanted to try a non hard case to avoid putting too much pressure on the joycons.
This one is definitely very soft and not too tight. The fit on the bottom and top could be a bit better. And the material is very slick. I recommend cleaning it before using for some reason it made it more grippy. Overall decent case and it does help protect a bit and ease hand pain while playing for an extended time. And if you use the dock often it's super easy to remove and replace so that's a plus.

title: Not perfect, but darn good.
text: I'm a little surprised that I like this product as much as I do. Typically I dislike Garnier products because of their scents, but this detangler has a mild scent that's not annoying in any way. It's easy to apply, and does help to detangle my hair and keep the frizz out of it. Even better, it doesn't make my hair oily and so doesn't mean I have to wash it every day. It's not perfect, but it's darn good.

You have to earn points to buy clues. If you run out you can buy them, so the clues are limited. The story was obviously built around the puzzles.

title: Loose Connection to Keyboard

This keyboard is brand new and the cable ending looks like it wasn't made very well. I often have to adjust the cable so it stops disconnecting over and over. I've tried using another cable and it works much better than this one.

title: Extremely Painful, Messy & Time Consuming
text:
It's AWFUL! Extremely painful. Worse than regular waxing. Messy! Messier than traditional waxing. I used it on my legs. I used to wax my legs regularly. After seeing the commercial it looked so simple, neat, and pain free. It's none of these good things! The wax doesn't come off as easily as it does in commercial. Unlike traditional waxing, when you can quickly rip the strips off in one motion, you can't do that with wonder wax. Which makes removal extremely painful, messy and time consuming.

DON'T BUY WONDER WAX unless your some type of masochist.

title: looks like the cheap scratched up plastic that it actually is
text:
I saw this in a YouTube review along with several other cases and figured for under $3 as an add-on item I'd give it a shot. Should have paid more attention to the negative reviews here on Amazon, because this thing is junk.

The "brushed metal" texture that looks so sleek in the product photos just feels awful to hold, looks like the cheap scratched up plastic that it actually is, and offers less grip than with the case off. Also the buttons are loose and literally rattle as you move your phone. My first and most likely last case from VRS/Verus.

title: Great for AV techs in a church, play, or other quiet setting
text: The AV team at our church need to click a lot on the computer during quiet moments in our service and this was PERFECT. It even takes some getting used to because you may think you didn't click on something because it didn't make a noise. This is a must-have. The cord was a little short since it needs to go under the counter to the computer so we used a USB hub on the desk. It would be fine for an application like that but if your computer tower is under the table you are using, you probably want an extension cable or a hub that sits on your desk.

Chop chop chop...uh-oh, I ran out of card to attack so I have to run around and reload to do a basic slash...no thanks! Why didn't they just keep the battle system the same? I liked my button mashing...Anyways in my opinion the battle system took much away from the actual game play and made me put the game down. I haven't played it since, and I prolly won't play it again.

Unless something changes soon (data intermittently won't work on either 5G or LTE) I will have to be switching back. I live in a major city, so the data coverage is not the issue.

title: Terrible product.
text: Don't waste your money on this. Took it out of the package, followed the instructions step by step and there are still tons of bubbles in the center and on the edge of it that won't come out no matter how hard we press. Also it doesn't fit the phone screen entirely there is a good quarter of an inch of space that it doesn't protect. Don't waste your money.

title: Nice lights but poor functionality
text: It was good for the first few days but then the keys started getting stuck by whatever plastic or screw that's propping them up. I tried breaking in the keys to loosen them up to no avail. Only positive is the nice light up feature.

title: Device doesn't show the USB stick even with debugging off!
text:
The device doesn't see the USB stick when I inserted it- it sees it in ES file app so I know my USB stick works. I turned of the debugging so it would read USB port but settings>device>storage doesn't show it. I think there is a problem with the latest software update.
The fire tv does show my SD card. However it ONLY seems to allow for app storage and nothing else.
Amazon fix the USB port please.

title: Buttons Too Flat & Yellow'd After a Month

title: Surprisingly good case for the price
text:
Pros:
* Great fit
* Has cutouts for the buttons, which is a must for me
* Looks surprisingly good - the metal fade look is much less cheesy than I expected.

Con:
* No dimple on the back for the Motorola logo, which I actually got very used to resting my finger on.

Very nice, cost effective package. However the game itself is no where near as good as Uncharted 2. The single player campaign was a huge let down from the previous installment and the online gameplay is completely broken and naturally unenjoyable.

Gone are the custom character models that you could replay the game as, as well as the option to load which ever weapon you wanted after completion of the game.

Still a very nice story on this one ... until you reach the ending which is anti climactic.

It is a great starter razor and has done well taking care of his thin facial hair, but I have had issues getting a close shave with foil razors in the past and some issues with skin irritation. For now though it is fantastic and meets the needs well. The motor and battery seem strong and even at the $150 price point, I think this is a solid razor choice. Recommend it!

title: Excellent quality as long as you do not need to transmit phantom power
text:
I am using this with a LyxPro 4-Channel pair of boxes connected via Ethercon. The quality of this cable is excellent, especially considering the lower price. It transmits noise-free too. And, finally, it is more flexible that I thought that it would be. If you are an old timer who remembers 10BASE2, this has about the same semi-flexible quality as that cable.

Unfortunately it will not transmit phantom power, and I suspect that is because one end of the ground is lifted. However, if you don't need phantom power this is a great value based on price, quietness and overall construction.

While that's great when you're driving -- no way the phone will fall out -- it's a real nuisance when you need to get going and instead have to fiddle with the mount to get the phone in or out.

title: Seems to do what the product is supposed to do
text: I use this product two times and it seems to work well. Usually have issues with reactions with some lotions, but not at all with this one.

title: Excellent value
text: This is my second purchase of this simple, 3-year calendar. I do a lot of planning over a year in the future, so this is perfect for me. No frills, but the size makes it easy to fit in my backpack, and I can see a whole month at a glance. I use it more than my phone for keeping track of my schedule! And it holds up to three years of use, too.

title: Absolutely The Best Electric Razor & Trimmer
text: I can’t say enough good things about this razor and trimmer. I am 62 years old, and this makes shaving my legs soo much easier. We go boating alot, sometimes on short notice, so I can grab my razor, and shave my legs in no time at all. Then my legs look great! The face trimmer is a must have…I had my eyebrows permanently tattooed on, so I can use it to remove hair. It also works great on other areas for removing hair. This shaver is cordless, rechargeable and I can use it with or with out water, and gel or lotion. You can shave any way you want with this awesome razor! I highly recommend it!

title: Cute idea but it REALLY needs to be weighted. ...
text: Cute idea but it REALLY needs to be weighted. I've been searching for magnets to attach to the bottom to weigh it down. As it stands, you have to hold it in one hand and pull tape with the other, or the shoe will pick up with it and you can't tear the tape off.

title: Yeah. I needed a third copy
text: I own two copies of this for the Wii. Part of my reasoning for the third was I owned the Zelda/leftwing Amiibo. Another was the option of non-waggle controls. It's thrilling doing qctual sword fights with the motion plus but I've got a very cuddly cat and have to cut strokes short for fear of hitting him.

title: Great sound, cheap plastic
text: I had this headset for about a year and no complaints with the sound quality or comfort. My problem is that these are made of cheap plastic and after a year of wear and tear the plastic weakend and one of the ear phones broke off.

title: Great action game
text: If you liked Half Life, Doom, Halo or Wolfenstein, you'll love this game. Just like all the others I mentioned, the game play is pretty much the same. The controls are pretty straightforward and the graphics are nice. The only downside is that you can't control the brightness which can be a problem at times, especially if you play during the day. During your different missions, you acquire various weapons to help you on your journey. This is a really great game and one to add to your collection.

title: Convenient but not effective for heavy odor
text: I’ve always had a problem with underarm odor. I’ve tried millions of deodorants (still on the search for my holy grail), and none of them hold up all day. I used these wipes as a midday refresh. I also tried them alone, first thing in the morning. I don’t think they remove all the odor and Bacteria because I can still smell myself. I can tell minimal difference after using. The rose scent is very light. For the cost, you’re better off with baby wipes. I will not reorder

title: Terrible.
text: This lagged and lagged and made game unplayable.

title: Cheaply made
text: Bought this when my other case broke. If your looking something durable I would not purchase this case. It is very cheaply made. The plastic kick stand is very cheap and will not hold up. I bought it to try because it came in orange. It does not come with a screen protector so there is no problem with the touch screen. Definitely not worth the price for the quality of the case

title: Tons of Spam McAfee pop-up ads
text: Constantly interrupted by irritating spam pop-up adds for McAfee products.

title: Fake. Don't waste your money. Go buy the real Dymo labels instead.
text: after struggling to try to figure out why all of my labels were falling off, I noticed that these aren't real Dymo labels! These things are cheap for a reason! Stay well away. These stickers start to fall off immediately, even on a clean/new surface. Spend the extra and get the genuine Dymo labels! Don't waste your money on these!

title: Conklin is Problematic on their Finishes
text: I got ths Amber Orange...nice looking considering I had to send the first one back due to a major color finishing flaw. I got the stub 1.1 mm nib. Hard to use recommend you get the broad nib. Also ordered another step up Conklin and yes had to return with no replacement a major finishing flaw. Went with a Faber Castell Loom matte finish broad nib that is far superior to the Conklin pens that have a terrible Quality Control Dept.

title: Flexible
text: This laptop case is very flexible. My first impression of taking it out of the box was that it wasn't sturdy enough to hold a laptop. This case measures for a 13.3" laptop, I placed my 12.3" laptop inside and surprisingly it was sturdy. The inside of the case is very soft. The outside is soft as well, but with a different texture than the inside. The zipper isn't revealed on the outside, when you zip it up, it's covered by the flaps. The handle is sturdy enough so that the case holds up well.

title: the display refresh rate is spotty at best
text: it has it's good moments of 30hz, but it declines to around 5hz or worse for some reason intermittently. maybe i got a defective unit, but my mouse can be working fine and then just go into major stutter mode with no understanding and it must be attributable to the monitor refresh rate. i think 30hz is the max, but it deteriorates rapidly while using. will probably need to return.

title: Another decent idea with poor manufacturing/QC.
text: I like the idea and design of the product but it's shortcoming is load bearing tack welds and incorrect hardware choices. This product could carry so much more weight if small changes were made. Fix the welds on these products and put some bigger screws in the package.

title: Not full like JBLs should be
text: Thin sound for JBL. Treble is dominant- little mid and very little bass. From JBL, I expect much fuller sound. It's an odd little speaker. Because if it's shake some kart is always pointing at the surface on which it is sitting. It's heavy, but solid. Connects without any trouble - Just turn both devices on - no having to switch off and on to find each other. Doesn't drop off. Cable is solid. If Harmon/JBL has a bass device that connects/pairs I may pick it up. This jack if full sound grates on me.

title: Four Stars
text: good for figuring out a little bit about muscle anatomy.

title: Great
text: Great case. Hard to get the clip open though.

title: Survives spider-induced throws!
text: After using the flashlight on my phone to navigate the basement, walked into a veritable tarantula, threw my phone in the air and watched it sail 6 feet into the air to smash down onto cement, suffered only a tiny crack to the screen protector, phone was fine! I admit, I was dubious when I put it on given how tiny it is, but knowing the damage done to batteries by the heat from improperly vented cases I thought this was a good compromise. I am very impressed with the product, would definitely recommend. I love that I can see the phone's natural color.

title: the battery and charger work flawlessly and ButterflyPhoto's service was excellent in getting a replacement
text: okay quality. I had to get a replacement for the car charger part as it literally fell apart in my hands. However, the battery and charger work flawlessly and ButterflyPhoto's service was excellent in getting a replacement.

title: Worked well until about the 11 month mark
text: This mouse was pretty good for just about a year and then it got super laggy and unresponsive.

title: Well made, but feels small
text:
I have a size large hand, not huge by any means, and this mouse for some reason was just so short feeling, my fingers felt like they were hanging off the end, and curling under the somewhat sharp edges of the L/R mouse buttons. Also, the buttons, although good, were just too small for my thumb. Overall, it is well made, can be both wireless, AND wired, which I did not know at first...I really wanted to keep this mouse, but oh well.

I settled for a Razor DeathAdder instead, which despite not having as many extra buttons, etc., fits in my hand like it was made for me.

title: It was great until it stopped working
text: The scroll wheel completely stopped working about 2 weeks after I got it, so I'm returning.

title: Writes well

title: Does not work well with cases.
text: Wanted this protector to work with my case installed but the edges would not stay down. Even bought a different case (Otterbox Defender) and it still doesn't stay on the edges. Unfortunately a vinyl screen like this just isn't compatible with cases because the case edge is too tight around the edges for it to stay intact. Bummer dude.

title: It beats spending a small fortune

title: Nice card

title: I love this mud mask
text: I love this mud mask! I immediately felt my skin minty and fresh when I applied this. It dries out within 10 minutes. Leaves your skin very smooth and refreshed. Best part, it has no bad smell. I got this with a discount.

title: Okay.....
text: I finally broke down and bought this product after wanting to try it for a year or so. Sadly, it was a bit disappointing. It did help brighten my skin a bit after a couple weeks, but it's super oily and leaves a shiny kinda silky appearance. As a guy this is a big no no. Needless to say I only use it at night. I Also found out it is only about 5% vitamin c. That's not much at all. Not sure if I'd buy again.....

title: Frozen, not the movie.
text: The screen is constantly freezing. I'm having to turn it back off, then turn it on in order to unfreeze the screen. I can't stand this, so it is being sent back.

title: Roomy laptop tote
text: I needed a good laptop bag to take to work and so I bought this one. I like the green inside and there's room for lots of things. The material of the bag is really durable, but the straps seem a bit thin and I'm concerned that in time through wear and tear they will split or rip while the rest of the bag stays in good condition. All in all, it's a good laptop bag (fits my big laptop), I can carry other things too (don't need to carry a purse) and it looks nice. I got some compliments on it.

title: Too Short! Way Too Shory
text: I was having fun with the game and figured I had completed maybe 10-15 percent of it. Then the credits rolled, I was shocked. Took a day and a half at most, that is all. Very disappointed.

title: Terrible
text: Difficult to dispense and the coverage is awful.

title: worked for iPhone
text: Convenient charging station for iPhone. It charged my phone with the protective case still on. It is a fast charge. My phone stayed in place with no slipping. I was hoping it would charge my non-apple phone and earbuds but it did not. That is on me. I also tried to charge my son's samsung phone and could not get it to work. If you are an apple person then this product is great. Works as expected and seems to be well made. It folds up for easy storage when not is use

title: Shame is doesn't look as good as XBox One

title: Budget chic

There is a shoulder strap included, and a few smaller pockets. The bag has a lived-in feel, like it already has a few miles on the road. Stick a copy of Kerouac in there and you're good to go.

title: I bought this again for the second time, having ...

And I finally agree with the people that say the expiration date must cause the blades not to work. hard to believe that steel has an expiration date, but you shave your legs and the next day need to again. I have tested this over and over.

title: zipper is not heavy duty

title: Replacing after 2 months: Broken already, Keys Worn off
text: I love the look of this product, but it only lasted two months under daily use on my MacBook Pro. I used the cover and keyboard cover. The keyboard cover immediately showed signs of wear as the ink started to rub off some of the keys. Then the cover started to chip away at the top center and middle bottom. The remaining shards of plastic are a hazard since it has cut my husband and I. The product is cheap and, although the design is cute, it was not worth it since I will have to replace it almost immediately after I bought it. Really Disappointed.

title: Great anti bacterial soap
text: This is a nice smelling antibacterial soap that lathers nicely. It doesn’t really list an ingredients list like other products and only shows six ingredients. If that’s the case, that’s awesome! The price point is right on for other soaps like this. It didn’t dry out my skin.

title: Not good. Try Ms. Pac-Man or Jr. Pac-Man instead.
text:
This is a pretty bad version of Pac-man. Very little in common with the arcade version. The sound is bad. The maze is different. The colors are wrong. Pac-Man only faces left or right (not up or down.)

Instead try Ms. Pac-man or Jr. Pac-man. They are closer to their arcade versions and much better than this cartridge.

title: Disappointing
text: After reading all the great reviews, I thought I had found reasonably priced, good sounding small speakers for an extension of my stereo system to a second room. All I can say about these speakers is, "You can hear the box." Anyone who has heard speakers that are acoustically invisible will know what I mean. These are definitely not in the premium quality category. I guess for the price, I should not have expected so much.

title: Expensive per ounce
text: I have been using pure Jojoba oil for years now. It's amazing stuff. The smallest amount goes a long way and it does a fantastic job of rehydrating itchy, damaged skin. And this product seems like the real deal. But why I am disappointed is because this little bottle costs a lot more than my go to brand. Almost four times as much per ounce! I normally do not comment on the price of a product, because value can be subjective. But as a frugal shopper, I can not highly recommend this particular product. The quality of the product is nice though, so I would still have to recommend the brand.

My fingerprint reader doesn't seem to recognize me anymore, so I'm working on how to get that to work even with the new screen protector. That's the only caveat - 9/10 stars!

The microphone wouldn't pick up my voice for some reason. Im not much of a talker so this didn't bother me too bad. Sadly my headset broke a couple days ago but unlike many other headsets I've had this one lasted the longest. I would definitely recommend this to others looking for a durable headset. Now im off to finish ordering this headset again. Hopefully it lasts as long as the last one :)

title: Great value for the money, leaves my skin feeling silky smooth!
text:
This Himalayan Salt Body Scrub has a pleasant Lavender small and a small tingling when applied. It doesn't really lather or foam up. It's more like a thick cream or lotion. Just washes off along with your dead skin cels and leaves you with smooth feeling skin after showering. After my first use my skin was already smooth and soft. The size of the jar is generous compared to others on the market. Overall WBM is a well known name for Himalayan Salt products , so I tried it.

If you find my review helpful, kindly click below so I may help others in the future as well.

title: Excellent little tablet

Now I'm back on the market for another mouse. *shaking my head in disappointment*

Not so much... I typically shave my legs every single day, year round, so I notice stubble immediately - hate that. The Embrace razor blade just doesn't get as close and it does leave stubble if you skip a day or two. I never had the before with three blade original. One good thing about the Embrace, the moisturizer strip is really good.

title: HDCP? This thing doesn't work with AppleTV
text: I'd never HEARD of HDCP until I got an error trying to use this little switchbox with AppleTV. No luck. Nothing happening. And my Sony TV has just exactly ONE HDMI jack. I am not amused in the slightest. Some greedy-lawyer-Hollywood "protection" scheme just deposited seagull manure on my home entertainment plans. It was only $16 but I'll bet that "HDCP" is nothing anyone wants or needs. And this thing can't handle whatever "HDCP" lawyer-talk requirements there are to FUNCTION. Save your cash.

title: Won't fit an older X200 or a newer X201 std lappy. Latch is too short. Nice vendor though.
text: These are crap batteries. The latch is made too short to latch and lock into either an X200 or X201 (not tablet) that we have. Tried it in another travel X201, same thing. Ordered another brand from another vendor. What came was yet another CBD battery. Identical latch, identical problem. Battery falls out of chassis if held with battery toward ground (whenever you slide it into a laptop sleeve or your backpack/briefcase. Will damage chassis eventually. The vendor was sympathetic though, said they'd refund return shipping. Seemed like nice people at least.

title: 100 Mbps on a gig LAN. Bummed.
text:
I had hoped for higher speeds than 100 Mbps. I have configured the devices according to the instructions, but they really are not any better than the WiFi in the area I am using them in. The internet connection at my house is 1Gbps.

They're not malfunctioning, but they are not what I had hoped for.

One tiny complaint is that there's no label Left / Right (the one with the knob is the right for me).

title: Covers well for me.
text:
This True Skin High Cover Concealer works great for me. I used it with my makeup and was very happy with the coverage. I wasn't sure about the color but it worked well for me.

This concealer comes in a long glass vial-type container. The applicator is a sponge and it works great to get the concealer on. The vial seems to have something that works like a spring to push the applicator up when you open the top.

I will be recommending this True Skin High Cover Concealer to family and friends.

The AmazonBasics mount does a much better job of this...the design is more well thought out. This one is fine if you never plan on adjusting the viewing angle of your tv, I guess. title: Not Happy...
text: Usual I have had good results with "Renewed Products", however this was the exception. Unit arrived one day late. When I opened the box I was happy to see the the unit was in great cosmetic condition. But when I connected the unit to my TV the sound would cut in and out every few minuets. To make sure it was not the TV at fault I connect it to another TV with the same result. I had noticed several different shipping labels on the box that indicated it had been returned at least once or twice before I got the item. So I am returning the defective unit to Amazon for a refund.

title: 13th planet...arbitrary puzzle solutions
text:
This is an old-style "adventure" game of the "director" sort, but with beautiful graphics. I just wish there were more games of this genre available. The price is right for this game.

The game's biggest defect is the arbitrariness of the puzzle solutions...there is unfortunately little logic or reasoning involved in some of them, like the "use banana with metronome" puzzle of an earlier era. It is therefore very important to save "early and often," to avoid being trapped by what appear to be irreversible detours down the wrong path. The game is definitely NOT non-linear.

title: Great face wash
text: I usually buy the Jack Black face wash for my brother, but wanted to try something different this time. Does not over dry skin, and pairs well with the corresponding facial moisturizer. The pump makes it very convenient to use, especially when your hands are wet and slippery. Overall, highly recommended.

title: Set up today 2022-06-26. Caution! Factory installed speaker wires set too far in: no sound.
text: So last thing to check if no sound but VU meters moving and headphones sound OK. Yes, the factory installed speaker wires were pushed too far into the connectors so the insulation prevented metal-to-metal contact. Solution: back out the speaker wires an reinstall. Just trying to be helpful. 2 days later: noted clipping and crackle in the FM radio at peak signal. As pointed out elsewhere, this is a bad FM radio. Occasional click/pop on the CD.

title: Would not import my Schwab transactions
text:
This is the second year in a row that Block was unable to upload my investment transactions. That forced me to buy TurboTax -- which could do the job. My TD Ameritrade transactions imported fine. Just not Schwab.

So, in my 20 years of using H&R Block software, it has failed me 3 times. That is unacceptable. TurboTax is more expensive, but more reliable. I'm done wasting my money on H&R Block.

title: Scattered income entry steps
text:
When entering business income, it says to EXCLUDE income which was reported on W2, so I did. Turns out there is another step where you are to enter W2 income, along with several obscure forms, and I missed it. Now have to do an amended return, and pay interest and penalty. IRS Schedule C, line 1 groups all business income together as it should. Why does TurboTax have to make it messy?
To their credit, they fixed a problem from last year, which failed to keep track of equipment depreciation from previous years, requiring digging up previous years' numbers, then manually entering the.

title: You may or may not get a good one.

Sometimes it would stop working correctly in the middle of a game. After I bent the pins a bit to one side now it seems to be okay. The controllers work fine with my NES system and its pins are centered. Either the pins need to be a slight bit wider to ensure a connection or there is an issue where the pins are soldered. It now works with my NES MAX, dog bone controller and original 1986 brick.

title: Just ok but watch out for the marker
text:
If you are at all sensitive to chemicals the marker is really strong! It has a very offensive chemical smell even after sitting on the paper. I eventually had to toss the drawing in a plastic bag and take it outside and I could still smell the marker in the room. And the marker was the main reason I bought the set.

If someone is brand new to CP art this might be nice especially for the price. The kneaded eraser is very tiny though and instead of a charcoal pencil a burnisher would have been better and more geared to colored pencil.

But really adorable and appealing for the front desk /admission that I'll say!

text: I buy these because they're cheap and they work. I have now purchased this item 4 times, and its protected my phone, and my girlfriends since we've gotten them (two plus years). Highly recommend. I haven't been able to find another item for the same price at this quality.

I suppose that would be okay if my account was a new one but it's not, and I need space to write four numbers. The Transaction Description space is small too, so unless I can come up with a "Plan B" these will be donated. I'm really disappointed!

title: Fast charge, but makes my phone lag a bit
text: These work good so far! I bought these because I like longer charger cords and because my other cables started to short out. I decided to purchase this particular one because there was a warranty. It seems like most chargers stop working after a couple months, so if that happens with these I should be able to get a replacement. The only thing i did notice with these is that they are a little harder to plug into my phone, and they do make my phone lag a bit when I have it on charge. But it does charge my phone pretty fast!

title: My battery never would hold a charge
text: I bought this thing years ago and forgot about it. The reason I forgot about it was from day one, I could never get it to charge. My guess is a faulty battery. I do like it and it does work if I keep it plugged in. I am not sure if there is a trick to charging it. Is the power switch supposed to be on when charging maybe? I don't know. It has been too long now to do anything about it. I am typing this review on it, but it has to be plugged in to work. I would have preferred it just take a AA or AAA battery. Rechargables suck sometimes.

title: Like anyone else
text: Finally! An affordable phone case that keeps my phone safe! Like anyone else, I love an Otterbox, but my wallet does not share the sentiment. This case is sleek, aesthetically pleasing, budget friendly and, most importantly, resilient. I have dropped my phone so many times that I've forgotten half of them, but this case has kept my phone safe during every single drop. I've dropped my phone on tile, pavement, rocks, and cement -- and my phone never cracked or got a scratch on it. I'm very happy with this purchase, and I highly recommend it.

The variety of layouts and the ability to shuffle tiles near the end is nice rather than losing. I would prefer a version with tiles that are a little more colorful an easily distinguished.

The Vidal Sassoon Hot Air Styler is the first of its kind that I've used so I can't compare it to others. This one feels kind of cheap but that is fine since it is not expensive. It takes a few minutes to get going, but I have the time. I find that it does take some coordination, but I usually have that, too. I like this little creation.

title: Looks Good, Works

These are a nice replacement for basic clear ones and they blend in with the corkboard a little which is nice.

Not sure if this is going to be fixed in a firmware update, but as of 9/23/2021 this is not possible with the latest firmware update from razers site.

title: Color is Mahogany
text: The color of this lipstick is deep mahogany. It smells ok, goes on very smoothly (it is a buttery formula), and the color is consistent. Using this without a lipliner will likely cause bleeding because of the softness of the lipstick. If you put this lipstick on your arm for a light swipe, it looks like there is a purple-ish hue to it, but after putting a full coat on, all you will see is a deep brown. The only skin this would look good in is a darker skin tone. Looks horrible on a person with fair skin (much too dark).

title: Nice concept, poor execution on the Fire
text:
This is a great concept for an app that blends written instruction with video to help teach basic yoga moves. Unfortunately, it's not very usable. The videos are just thumbnail sized and very hard to see. The audio is poor and almost impossible at times to understand, even with an external speaker. There's no way to play routines in sequence - you have to select them one at a time which is very cumbersome if you're trying to use it while actually doing the moves.

It's worth a look if you can get it as a free app to review some basic yoga moves/poses. Otherwise, don't bother.

title: The Good, the Bad, and the Ugly - Saved net $1 per month
text:
The Good: Easy setup in 5 minutes. Saves me $13/mo. Xfinity router rental. (but... read on)
The Bad: Shorter range / weaker signal compared to my old Xfinity router. Poor signal on the deck and in the backyard. (Never a problem with my old Xfinity router.)
The Ugly: Often disconnects my laptop and iPhone Xr. Takes 45-60 seconds to reconnect. Causes my iPhone to use Xfinity mobile data so it goes over the free 100 Mb monthly data. Gets charged $12/mo. per Gb.
The End: So overall, I saved $1 per month. Woohoo.

title: Great bargain.

This bag is made for small mirrorless or compact cameras only. I returned it and getting a bigger one. Careful when buying by checking the size against what you have. I'm sure it's good for someone.

title: Not told that Steam was required
text: I play most simulators solo and have no interest in using Steam's collaborative features. When I tried to install the game on my PC, it would not let me proceed without entering a Steam account. Nowhere in the product description does it mention this requirement. I think that this is bad faith and am returning it.

title: Won't flatten out. Rough texture that is uncomfortable.

The pad is very abrasive and feels like moving my wrists across sandpaper.
I don't recommend this a a desk pad.

The pad does seem thick and provides protection. It would probably work great for other purposes if it wasn't coming into contact with skin.

Also, if you aren't careful you can initiate the pump too easily. Just make sure you have a cotton ball or remover pad over the opening to avoid the liquid from spurting out. The remover is great & works quickly, just think the bottle needs work.

I am not pressing hard and do not have that problem with a less expensive brand! I cannot get my money back but hopefully can protect you from buying this product!

title: the facial oil works well

title: cheap quality

title: Made in China. USB-A and USB-C Nice combination.
text:
These dual-purpose AC adapters are nice when. . . .like me. . . you have a “newer” phone which can charge fast. . . but. . . you have other devices which still use USB-A type charging.

If you’re placing this into a surge protector. . . It depends upon your protector and how the prongs go into the protector as to whether or not these will take-up “extra” room. If that’s an issue, you might want to check first before purchasing.

These chargers are Made in China and being sold by a Chinese company on Amazon.

title: Love these!
text: Have tried so many wireless earbuds but none of them would stay in my ears, I have small openings. Well, my nephew told me about these and I'm so happy. Took me a minute to figure out how they went into the ears, but once I figured that out it was great. You do have to remove them from the earbud to place back in charger case, but that's not a deal breaker. I've tilted my head side to side, open and closed my mouth, even sneezed and they still stayed in place. I'm a huge fan and love that I can wear earbuds now.

title: Just as described!

title: Top quality

title: Writing is silver foil. Made in China.
text:
These mini 2.5” X 3.5” cards / envelopes are Made in China. What doesn’t show very well in the pictures is the fact that the lettering on the outside is silver foil, so it shines, which is really nice.

The inside is blank. That might be an “issue” for some kids, as they will be expected to write something on the inside.

At the current price of fifteen bucks for Made in China cards. . . I think it’s quite expensive. But . . . as is usually the case with Hallmark. . . they are of better quality than many other cards.

title: Functionally the same as lower cost brushes with boar bristles.

It is made in the USA.
The rating reflects the fact that it is functionally the same as low cost boar bristles with plastic handles.
My wife prefers the lighter inexpensive plastic handles with boar bristles. This brush feels heavy.
After two uses, the bristles showed wear.

Best used for someone with thick hair.

The idea of a reusable (fabric) box is somewhat interesting. Most all of my regular boxes get reused over and over. Some are at least 20 years old. So, the reusable factor isn’t a BIG deal.

For almost $24, this gray fabric box is snazzy enough to be in a living room or home office, even a bedroom. It isn’t heavy duty though. If you have hanging files, this won’t work that well either.

It comes with ONE “see-through” inventory card pouch. Unless you’re close to it, it won’t be seen.

This isn’t something I would get again or recommend.

CONS:
MADE bumps on my face! - Not like acne, but just some ITCHY bumps. My skin felt LESS smooth.

SMALL container for the money - I even got on sale, but it's very small. You don't need much at all, so I guess it would go far...IF it worked for me, but didn't.

title: WARNING: YOU MUST Have a PAID Playstation Plus Account or This Game Will NOT Work!
text:
WARNING: YOU MUST Have a PAID Playstation Plus Account or This Game Will NOT Work!

NOTE: My 1-Star review has noting to do with the actual game play.

My 5 year old kept begging to get this game for him. We didn't realize you must have a paid playstation plus account. They should put it in bold on the description with a big warning that you must have a PAID playstation plus account. Not everyone with a PS4 even knows what playstation plus account is.

title: A slow phone that takes multiple taps.
text: The phone is okay. I'd give it a score of 8.5. It is a SLOW phone and usually takes multiple taps to register a function. If I remember correctly, I had to go through the settings to put the back and close out to the home screen and see previously opened apps. What I will also tell you is that I read the reviews on the phone to make sure I was making a good choice. Even after doing several taps, it can take awhile for it to open! I'd suggest that if you can spend a little more, you'll probably get a better phone.

title: Not worth it
text: This is no better than the ones you can get a three pack of for $7. It is guaranteed, but when it cracks, it costs $7 for them to ship the replacement, which is what you would pay for 3 of the cheap ones. Also, it takes 16 days to receive the replacement. It is a good screen protector, but not any better than the less expensive options.

title: Impressed!
text:
I am super impressed with the latest gen version of Echo dot. I have paired these speakers as a stereo and also I am able to make these speakers work as a hometheater for my firestick.

There are no issues with lip sync or delay in audio as it uses the wireless network. I have always had issues with Bluetooth. This works perfectly for my projector, no cables for audio required.

Will try to add a sub to my setup soon.

title: Mildly exfoliating sponges but too large for me to get a good grip
text: I wanted to love these sponges but I do not. There are two in the package and they arrive extremely compressed. I just added water and they expanded as shown in my photo. The sponge is mildly exfoliating due to the rough exterior. However, I find these sponges difficult to wash with because they are bigger than my hand. For me this is a big disappointment. Also, the current price of $9.99 is an expensive $5 per sponge.

I was disappointing with this pen. I write with fountain pens exclusively, and this is not a great pen. It's not BAD, but it's not great. Very flimsy feeling, cheap. It took forever for the ink to come down to the nib, and it writes just as good as a dollar store pen.

But if you are looking to give a fountain pen to a true newbie or a kid, this may be good.

The only thing I dislike is that the front of it is sloped. My wrist rest kinda rides up on it. This is a minor gripe though and actually might force me the have better typing posture anyway.

title: Ok but not great

Two of the pens have good flow with nice lines (and just fine for lefties). One of the set is not smooth and seems uneven and scratchy. The tip doesn't look perfect so that may be the issue. For $12 I don't think it's a great deal either. So this isn't "the" pen. On the plus side no smearing and ink dried right away. Very dark ink too.

title: Low quality construction, poor welds
text:
I was really disappointed in this item. While it is generally functional and "OK", the welds that hold the baskets together are done poorly to where the baskets look uneven, bent, and not straight. It is particularly obvious on the top basket, which is the most visible, that the center weld has a distinct angle to it rather than being straight and neat.

While the welds are strong and the item functions as intended, it is hard to recommend given the lack of quality and attention to detail.

title: Well.... I have Netflix as well ...
text:
Well....I have Netflix as well, and to my mind Amazon's service does not compare well. The "free" offerings seem very limited in comparison, and on the occasion that I was re-watching a series (like the Sarah Connor Chronicles) , you took it off streaming when I was about halfway through.

I would rather see either a flat monthly fee or a slight increase to the "Prime" fee and more streaming availability.

No problem with the quality of the streaming service...Pictures are sharp and clear on the computer or the TV. But Netflix remains our go-to.

title: Excellent treatments for winter skin!
text: I used the hand mitts and let me tell you -- just wow! I live in Wyoming and the winters here are long and hard and always reek havoc on my skin. My hands are usually the worst and I'm always on the look out for a way to help protect them and treat them. These mitts *really* helped return a ton of moisture to them! The face masks were no different, except for being used on my face instead of my hands lol. They also put a lot of moisture back into my facial skin and made my skin pre-winter soft again!

title: working WEMO plugs
text: My review is for the app and the plug outlets. I setup two of these in my house the other night. Setup was pretty quick and easy. I ran in to a few little quirks but got through them. As most reviews have stated, there are numerous updates to be done. After all that, I set them up on my Amazon Echo, made a group, and now can my living room lamps off or on individualy or at the same time. I can also do it from my phones or iPad. Love it so far.

I also have the Dancing With The Stars Wii game; but that one is not user friendly and the music isn't as fun as this one.

title: Mediocre speakers
text:
Sound is ok unless you want it louder (about 60-70% of the volume control range) , then you can clearly hear a distortion on the middle and high frequency. It holds the lower range pretty good. The volume control knob is small, tight and slippery. It's hard to turn or rotate it without holding the speaker. Price/quality ratio is good, that's why 3 stars.
There's no output for headphones.

title: Great skin.
text: I had my doubts when I ordered this. It did take a few minutes to figure out how it was suppose to go on. My whole living room is dragon themed, and before any single you live at home mom comments, I do have a wife and she loves dragons and video games as well, lol. It looks much better around some of the statues I have instead of just the plain look. I will be looking into a dvd player skin now. What I like about it, is that you could place and peel until you got it just right without it loosing it stickyness.

title: Nice color and layout, but wide keys feel dull
text:
I bought this keyboard because of its size. I love the form factor and the RGB colors. It actually has mixes of RGB, so you can see fuchsia, lime green, sky blue, etc.
But equally important is the feel of the keys. While most keys are fine, the wider keys (space, enter, shift, backspace) feel dull. Not sure if it's because I'm used to the Cherry MX (I had red switches before), or if it's just the build quality.
It's a shame. I really wanted to like this keyboard. It was just the right budget, size, and colors.

title: Cannot adjust size
text: Bought these for my 13 year old for school. He likes them, says they look cool, and they work well. So far, though, we have not been able to adjust the size to make them smaller. They fit, but not as well as if they band was adjustable. This lessens the amount of noise cancellation. Probably great for someone with a large head.

title: Solid Game
text: I recently started Ryse and I'm enjoying it so far! I have only gotten my feet wet so far but a lot of the reviews stating it is repetitive and short are spot on. Dealing out a good death is fun but you must do it over and over again. They get mixed up a bit and are fun to do when you hit the right combo. But the levels seem short, I breezed through 3 in no time at all. The voice controls with the Kinect is fun, nice being able to call in a strike when able too. I'm looking forward to finishing the game and then seeing what multiplayer has to offer.

title: Thuesday & Thurthday stink
text:
Well, what can you say about a calendar the has Thuesday & Thurthday? Yep, that how the two days are spelled on the calendar.

While it rips my OCD apart my wife thinks it's hilarious.

Not to be too picky but I expect the week days to be spelled correctly on something that is a calendar and I pay for.

It works, I guess. Is what you would expect.

title: GPS 7" screen review

I want it for its primary purpose and I know that some others (more expensive) have other bells and whistles that I don't need, understand or want to understand. If you want to go from point A to point B, this will get you there and if you need almost anything answered while you are on the way, this unit will answer it. The other things the more expensive units have are geekish to me.

title: Just A Mediocre Phone

This phone is OK. Like most feature phones, it has a great battery life, but the screen is low res and the 2mp camera takes pretty bad photos. The small tictac keys are hard to press if you have larger hands (you will get two or three keys at a time). The touch screen is not so sensitive and hard to use.

The voice quality is good, though. This might be a good emergency phone but it would not be a good phone for someone with larger hands.

Update- I'm averaging a refill every 3 weeks in my four pens in use at work.

title: Nice little viewer

Although it does not come with instructions, there are countless images on the net for reference. I did end up using glue to make sure the lenses and some Velcro straps are secure. Also, it is a little small for my Samsung Note III so I had to fiddle the flap and glue some extended padding for easier strapping. Overall, it is hard to fault the low price.

title: Nice basic fabric insert case
text: This is a nice basic value case assuming it’s in the $8-$13 range, but I can’t see the price as of this review. It protects against minor scrapes and tumbles. Easy to grip because of the material, the finish is fabric on the top, and it looks nice! The charging port, light, and cameras are all accessible and unobstructed, and you can easily change the case out. Over time, it will probably loosen and become kind of easy to take off, maybe a bit too much so, but right now it’s fine, so maybe it will last. See pics for close up of cloth.

title: Copious suds & cleansing but burns eyes & aroma almost like Play-Doh to me
text:
This body wash produces a very good lather and feels cleansing. However, I do not care for the aroma, which reminds me of Play-Doh. Also, it burns the eyes. I accidentally got some suds in my eyes and did not like the sting.

The current price of $26 is also pricey for 13 ounces. If I loved the aroma and it didn't burn my eyes , I would have felt as if I was having a spa experience and rated this 5 stars.

title: Works great

title: Balances combo skin
text:
I was excited to try the Georgette Klinger Oil-Free Skin Balance Gel because I can't stand the greasy residue of most hydrating skincare products.

At my age, I should moisturize more often than I do, but parts of my skin still tend to get a bit oily, so I tend to shy away from many of the serums that add shine or feel tacky.

This lightweight gel locks in moisture after cleansing. It isn't deeply hydrating by itself, but my complexion feels softer, smoother, and more balanced after using it.

title: Overpowering Fragrance!

title: More subscription than software

The good news: If you have the right browser and this will run on your version of Windows, it's a great starter set for video editing beginners. My son loves editing videos and my daughter is practicing to be a streaming star (she's not old enough to even have a YouTube channel, but she practices as if she did).

The bad news: if you don't have the right browser or the right operating system, this program is useless. It's more a subscription to a cloud service, not software.

title: does not work on older Samsung DLP TVs. i ...
text: does not work on older Samsung DLP TVs. i have a (HLS5086WX) and this does not work. I get an "Unsupported signal" error. Apparently the older version of this had a button to switch between 720 and 1080 output. Now it tries to detect this from the TV and apparently doesn't always work. So if you TV maxs at 720p then stay away from this and get one of the many other versions that still have a manual switch to force 720p mode.

title: Good quality 20Ah battery

It will output while charging, which is always a win. For others that use magnetic charger tips - they work well on this; I did remove the USB cover for convenience.
Protip - do NOT use the solar charging! I've destroyed so many good batteries by leaving them in direct sunlight. These tiny solar cells never give much power and baking your high capacity battery in full on sunlight is a horrible idea.

title: No!
text: I bought this to compare with another pad we had. First issue, we had to let it sit for 3 days to open fully. Neither me, nor my husband, like this cushion. Second (and main reason we don't care for it is) it smushes flat as a pancake when you sit on it and it slides all over the chair. Who wants to keep repositioning their pad ??? My recommendation is to spend a couple more bucks and get a seat pad that will stay in place and not smush flat as a pancake. We threw out the packaging or we would be sending this back. Instead, it will be donated.

title: I couldn't use these

I noticed earlier this week that my watch fell off in the car while wearing it. I am just thankful it happened in the car so I didn't lose my Apple watch. I know it just may be me as I am not super handy with this kind of stuff. Just think that, as with other watch bands. this shouldn't be as complicated as this is.

title: Nice for camping

Takes a few hours to charge via usb (charge before you go) and it I ever get lost in the woods I'll try the solar panel. It has nice sturdy case with grips and seems solid, not cheap. When all the LED lights are blue and not blinking it's ready to go.

title: Better options available
text: You are better off getting a high end pack of rechargeable batteries, you can find them on Amazon for $20, where you get 4 AA batteries AND the charger. I would recommend those over this because this battery pack will die a lot quicker and you will find yourself using the charging wire more and more. The wire included isn't special either, it's a standard usb to micro usb wire, you can find that also on Amazon for $5. Overall you are better off getting some good rechargeable batteries for $20 (you'll get 4) and spending another $5 on a usb to micro usb wire.

title: McAfee Does The Job!!!
text:
McAfee Total Protection 2015 was very easy to install. The thin packaging material contains a small card with a series of numbers. These numbers must be typed into McAfee's website. Thereafter, you simply follow a few more instructions and you're done.

This process was quick and very easy. And, once installed, this product did not slow down my computer.

So, I'm very glad that I'm using McAfee again to protect my computer.

title: poor performance on USB-C connection
text: I tried using this cable on a few different devices. It does work for charging and data connectivity, but performance is mediocre. It does not fast charge my phone, which uses USB Power Delivery (like many modern phones and laptops). Data transfer is at USB2 speeds, not the much faster USB3 standard. This cable is fine as an emergency backup, but performance is too mediocre for me to use it regularly.

Pros
Compared to the older model, this one looks so much cooler, and feels great.

Cons
The pointy part at the bottom is shorter and feels more stubby compared to the older model.

Overall

title: So painful to wear
text: I don't have complaints about the sound. I thought the sound quality was pretty good. But, because I work in an open-space environment at work, I need earphones that I can wear for long periods of time—these are not comfortable. After about a half hour, my ears were in so much pain that I gave up and went back to my old pair that didn't have quite as good of sound to them. The bluetooth connection is a bit wonky and sometimes disconnects for no reason. My other headphones never had this problem. I do not recommend these at all.

I will not be recommending this Hair Brush to family and friends.

title: Buy only at Amazon, and cancel the auto-renewal!
text:
Norton's okay. It's a bit intrusive, but I guess most of them are.
It's very easy to install on multiple devices, but it does tend to consistently bog processing times on day-to-day functions...honestly it can be a bit frustrating.
Here's the biggest drawback: Auto-renewal.
Tip: The moment you buy it, log onto the Norton website and CANCEL the auto-renewal!!!
If you don't you'll pay as much as 2x to 3x the Amazon price and...
Norton DOESN'T remind you ahead of time about renewing your subscription.

I even tried to put one on a small twist on my hair (which is looser than a braid so its more malleable to fit through the jewelry), but I had a hard time getting it on. Also, it got tangled in my twist (by the spirals on the end) and was hard to get out. Also, the color looks a little faded already. Wouldnt recommend this item.

title: Only plays Region 1 discs
text:
Works well. I just wish it could play foreign discs. It's coded to only play movies from Region 1 (USA), so I'm out 30 bucks since it won't play movies from Region 4 (I didn't know about all this "Region" crap until now). From what I've read online, cheaper dvd players make them able to play ALL regions, so why SONY had to go and limit theirs is pretty annoying. I also read that even if you get your dvd player to play a region 4 disc, a sony tv most likely won't play it for you. Wonderful.

Thanks Sony, gonna keep this in mind next time I'm in the market for any dvd players and tv's.

title: Looked like a frosted silver color to me!
text:
I’m not into frosted nail polish, but I was courious about Paris & Vine from the reviews here.
The polish goes on smooth, but to my eye this was Silver and Not a rose gold color that I was expecting.
I usually wear Opi polish, and the paris & vine polish seemed to dry as fast as Opi.
Over this color I use Opi RapiDry, and it went on fine and dryed fast.

The large square black lid top was too big and would not open this polish , it just kept spining around, and came right off to expose
A little lid cover that I had to open this with!

title: The repetition is helpful, but the sentences are silly ...
text: The repetition is helpful, but the sentences are silly. Many of them don't make any sense and some of them would never be used in anyone's native tongue let alone a foreign language. Customer service is non-existent. I've submitted two questions trying to understand the progress bars and the meaning of the various colors and no one has responded. That was a week ago. Finally, because of the nonsensical sentences, answers are often right, but scored as wrong. Answers match the drop down options, but they're scored as incorrect. All that said, it's worth the price!

title: You can have bright and shiny without fingerprints
text: I have gone through many cases. Some that I loved initially, I grew to hate. Not with this one, I am still loving it. My husband needs a case that is rugged but not too bulky. This fits his needs perfectly. The fact that you don't get fingerprints on it is a big plus. The fact that all the ports are accessible (as they should be) is another plus. The fluted inside keeps your phone protected with shock absorption dispersion. This is important if you drop your phone. It feels good in the hand and he is very happy with this case.

It does feel a little fragile, and I’m not sure if will last long without coming apart. But I don’t plan to use it often, so it’s ok.

title: Music at my fingertips.
text: I really enjoy this new device. All I have to do to hear a wide variety of music anytime is turn on the TV, hit the HDMI sign and speak into the speaking device. Who would have thought. I have listened to recordings I have not heard in years, some of which I own but would have to spend hours searching. And then there are the thousands of recordings I do not own, immediately available.

title: Too many notifications
text: Just let me play the stupid game!! Quit bugging me with hints or telling me how many coins I've acquired. Every 20 seconds I get interrupted by some info I already know. At least make it possible to turn it off. I like word searches, but I Hate getting interrupted for useless reasons.

So happy with this purchase--it was pretty cheap for the amount in the tubes and the pay-off was definitely worth it! The girls used the colors all over and there is still a ton of paint left. If you have a night color run, a blacklight dance, or some other reason to want to show off and glow in the blacklights, these are a great addition!

title: Good as a Pre-Styler for Volume and Shine
text:
This product is pricey, but if you need a pre-styler that adds volume and natural shine, it’s decent.

I am not much for the pomade-looking hairstyles, which this will definitely give you when styling, so I use this as a pre-styler and it works pretty well. The product smells fresh and beachy, and the consistency is like a more-elastic pomade. I apply it before blow-drying and it adds some volume and shine to my hair. I then style with a clay and the results are not bad.

title: Story sucks. No map editor. No SDK. Maps are too small

I really like the option to make contracts and challenges for the world and for your friends. Overall I was unimpressed with the game. Buy it when its heavily discounted!

title: Provides good powder blush/foundation coverage; bristles do not shed; decent grip; handle smudges
text:
These brushes are very nice. They provide good powder blush and powder foundation coverage. I prefer it for setting (a.k.a. finishing) powder or powder bronzer. I also love that the synthetic bristles do not fall out. The only negative is that the tiny nub of a handle is harder to hold than a typical foundation brush but it still provides a decent hold due to the thumb indentation. The bulbous handle base is hollow as seen in my photo. Since the handle is glossy, it does show all make-up smudges.

The current price of $6.99 comes to $3.50 each brush which is competitively priced.

title: There are better blues
text: While this is a nice color, it won’t last. I have been wearing “fashion” colors for upwards of 10 years. I’ve tried every brand on the market. Recently the shade I wanted was out of stock in another brand so I figured I’d try manic panic again since it’d been a long time. I quickly remembered why it’d been so long. Most of the colors I use I can get a good month, sometimes 2 out of at least and that’s washing my hair 2-3 times a week. This one looked awful after the first wash. It just has no staying power.

title: Relaxing but way too expensive
text: You get one each of four different types of mask (calming, brightening, moisturizing and rejuvenating) making it a great starter kit. The masks smelled nice but fit my face kinda funny. They have slits to allow for different face sizes and shaped but I couldn't get them flat against my face.. ended up with multiple ripples. Still felt relaxing though. Overall, while these masks due smell good and were relaxing, I won't be a repeat customer - they are just too expensive compared to many other available options on the market.

title: A lot that I like
text: There is a lot that I like about this NODOCA Arm Rest and Desk Extender. I like that it isn't too big. It's heavy and well built and comes with a case, silicone adapter for 2000 and 3000 series PSPs and a microfiber cleaning cloth. On the downside, it gets a little fatiguing to hold after awhile. I’m not sure if this is a ‘marketing in different countries’ thing, so I’m going to try it on my skin. If it doesn’t feel the same... my review will stand and I’ll return it.
If it does feel the same... I’ll update my review.

title: Not worth my money

text:
Followed the steps to connect device to my laptop and the website it sent me to to download gave me a warning that my computer's safety has been compromised giving me a virus. HORRIBLE EXPERIENCE. Never have I ever experienced anything like this with another printer.
My new laptop is ruined.

title: Review for Burning Clay
text: I ordered the Focallure 3 piece contour set and really liked it, so I was excited to add another blush color. Like the other sticks, this is easy to use because the color is buildable and blends so easily. This came in a sealed box in a sealed bag with a plastic cap under the lid. I didn't notice any scent. The only minor thing is I thought Burning Clay would be more terra cotta, but it's more warm rose. It suits me, but I was hoping for something with more of an autumnal orange to it. I hope they will offer such a color. This is well priced at $9.99 plus an extra 5% off.

title: Not for me
text: Didn't have the channels that I was looking for.

title: The brush is too fluffy to work. The product ...
text: The brush is too fluffy to work. The product is also drier than other mascaras I've used. It was hard to get a lot of coverage.

title: A huge step backwards but not a bad option if you mostly play handheld
text: The thick rubbery finish makes it difficult to dock and the included matte strips only make me wonder why they didn't just give the case a matte finish. The older models for the original switch were more comfortable, easier to slide on, and easier to dock. The trigger and shoulder pads make the triggers feel more pronounced but it also makes the buttons noticeably more stiff. This product feels like a huge step backwards. At the very least, the grip feels like it supplies some protection for the console.

title: Scent is WAY too strong.
text: Bought this because hemp oil had been great for my skin in other lotions, but the scent in this lotion is so strong it gives me a sore throat and a headache.

title: Nice charger with a display and multiple input ports
text:
Nice charger that's able to charge batteries individually. The screen also gives an indication on how much the charge is currently at. Much better than other chargers with blinking lights.
My only complaint here is that the batteries are held in really tight and its hard to take them out.

title: Short shelf life

title: Not a Herman Miller, but good for the price
text: The only thing I dislike about working from home is that I miss my Herman Miller chair. Sigh. Anyway, I don't have like a grand to drop on an office chair and my vintage tanker chair started to kill my back. I took a chance on this and my back issues are gone (I don't have a serious back issue just fyi). The lumbar support is just hard plastic against the back webbing, so it's not cushy or anything, but the seat is. All in all I'm really happy with it so far (about a month in).

But the new Kindle still won't pair with my old Bluetooth keyboard. And I have no interest in typing with two fingers on a virtual keyboard. Alexa stinks.

title: Avoid this game...
text: Nowhere near the content necessary to justify the price. A simple and repetitive game built, basically, on a mobile game structure. You will perform the exact same actions repeatedly ad nauseum with no potential for new elements. (Think ascendant shards vs. radiant shards.) Very disappointing considering the hype. This game is the precise reason I'll never preorder another game. Preordering allows piss-poor developers to make their expected profit margins before allowing their games to face scrutiny. title: Not Enough Results to Justify the Price
text:
For the price of one ounce of HydroPeptide Power Luxe Cream, I expect to be blown away by it.

I'm not.

Don't get me wrong, this is a great nighttime cream. I appreciate that it contains Bakuchiol rather than Retinol which tends to irritate my sensitive skin. It definitely delivers deep, lasting hydration and does help reduce my fine lines. But I've experienced no 'youthful glow to the skin', plumping or firmer skin.

I do not experience enough positive results to justify the cost of the cream. I have no plans to purchase it in the future.

When he doesn't need the mic, it does easily flip up and out of the way, which is convenient for him. This headset will work for him for now, but I imagine as he gets more serious into his gaming, he'll opt for a sturdier headset that does more to block out the household noise around him.

title: Too short!
text: How long is a 6 inch cord? One might suspect that the answer is “6 inches you big dummy!” But alas, thats not the case. In the real word, cords are measured from the inside of the connectors. These cords are distinctly 5”. Were you to include the connectors, you would be at 7”. There is no 6” rational measurement. That makes it a tiny bit too short to charge an ipad.

title: Not Cat Friendly
text: Extremely pugent. I had to throw these out after two days, because my cat started to sneeze really bad. Then I learned that lavender oils are toxic to cats. I've used other lavendar soaps and shampoos with no problems. However, I don't recommend this product for cat owners.

I find that it's a nice treat and I have to make sure I don't use it too often.
I do like that it has KSM-66 for ashwagandha as it makes for a more trusted ingredient source where the ability to consistently produce a product for its' effectiveness (rather than sourcing raws from different areas and hoping for the best, unless also receiving 3rd party testing).
Really great product with many health benefits.

title: Lightweight with very limited protection, if any
text: This is a very light case, which is the primary reason I purchased it. However the 'protection' that it offers for your laptop is very minimal. The padding in the laptop section is about 1/8th inch / 3mm max (see photo). The elastic strap that holds the laptop in the section is not adjustable, so it cannot be pulled tight (see photo). And while there is some additional padding at the bottom of the laptop section, it is extremely thin and does not stay aligned with the laptop (thin black strip in photo). As such, the laptop is completely unprotected at its most vulnerable point.

title: Good keyboard, key caps could be better.
text:

My issue with the keyboard for the $250 price. The key caps feel janky and not to quality and not so much a premium feeling.

The ghub is horrible software. Overall the keyboard can better key caps if you’re going to charge $250.

I’ll stick with my huntmans elite for now until Logitech fixes quality issues on their end.

title: A little disappointed
text: I was a little disappointed with Super Monkey Ball. It has a main game, and 50 mini-games. The main game is quite fast paced and fun, however, it is a little too simplistic to justify the cost of a full game. Supposedly the 50 mini-games should make up for it. However, these mini-games are so difficult to control to be any fun. After playing Wii Sports, I just cannot get used to the awkward gaming experience Super Monkey Ball has to offer.

title: Looks Neat
text:

The headphones use all plastic construction and are pretty stiff. The padding has a very slick, synthetic feel to them that is uncomfortable to wear for long periods and irritates my ears. While the price is pretty cheap I really expected better quality.

title: Good PCIe To USB Adapter Without Supplemental Power
text:
I bought this specific PCIe to USB adapter because I saw it doesn't need additional power from a sata power / molex power cable.

I used this to go to my Fiio E10k and it works great. My motherboard's USB ports were loud and had a lot of electrical interference.
When I plugged my DAC into this I was amazed at how quite it was.

I also used it to plug in my microphone's interface.

It is very small and compact as well.

title: Wi-Fi was incredibly slow compared to the XR500 AC2600!
text: The setup was fairly easy if you use the cell phone app, and the signal was strong for the hard-wired devices. However, the Wi-Fi output was poor, maybe 60 Mbps, compared to the 200 Mbps I was getting on my Netgear XR500 AC2600. After a couple of hours of running comparisons, I was not impressed with this router and decided the extra money for the XR500 AC2600 was worth it for me, simply because of the great Wi-Fi output.

text: I’ve used touch up powders in the past to fill in a few thin areas when wearing a pony tail so I thought I’d give this a try. It didn’t really do anything. It didn’t stick or even look like I used anything at all. If you are a clean Ingredient person, this isn’t for you. The case is really nice that has a magnet to close. I also love the “hello beautiful” on the mirror. I wasn’t impressed by this.

title: I really use my Kindle app and like it very much
text: I really use my Kindle app and like it very much. I can read everything from books to crochet patterns. Easy to use.. I recommend it if you haven't tried it. Nice thing about Kindle is that it can be on all your devices and you can take it anywhere you go. Love it!

title: a good option for cable-cutting
text: Overall, a good option for cable-cutting. I noticed it on the channel lineup in Amazon TV and decided to try it. You get a bunch of channels (many in HD) that seem like they are reposts from other feeds (CBS News, CNET, etc.), but all in all, it's pretty good fare. I do notice that on my Amazon Fire Stick, it tends to stutter a lot and wish I had a way to increase the buffering.

title: Wonderful Collection, But Missing the Crown Jewel
text: Wonderful games. Loved all 3. Good stories, fun gameplay, good characters, beautiful world's, etc. In this collection , AC Blackflag is the best. However, AC Rogue is missing from the series. Rogue is my favorite in the Americas saga. It ties off the Fenway family story and leads right into the AC Unity story. Not sure why they would release only 3/4 of the Americas saga in a collection. They should have waited until Rogue was out to do it right.

title: Good quality sound but....
text:
I wanted to listen to music while I did worked on my computer,, without disturbing others. These had a couple of issues:
It locked the computer tab you were working on, and you couldn't open a new tab.
The volume control was not easy to use. It was either too high or too low. Unreliable to control volume.
The fit really wasn't that comfortable.
If those issues don't bother you, they have a really nice quality sound, and good bass. The cost was right.

title: Value for price, but definitely not a name brand tracker

title: Fiskars take-apart shears
text: I bought 2 pairs of kitchen shears: one pair had black handles and the other pair was the orange-handled Fiskars. The shears with the black handles couldn't even cut through chicken skin, let alone small bones. I'll be using those out in my garden from now on. The Fiskars take-apart shears are sharp and cut through meat and other food with ease. They also come apart so they can be washed completely clean. I like that idea when I've been cutting raw meat. It's easy to put them back together. I'd buy another pair of these in a heartbeat.

title: pain in the ear
text: To activate this ear set when you receive a call, you must press on the hidden switch in the face. IT IS NOT A TOUCH PLATE, but a pressure switch. To get the switch to click requires considerable force, and shoves the earpiece into your ear. This is very uncomfortable. Do the manufacturers actually test these devices? Once the thing is turned on, it works well enough. But this design defect offsets any advantages. I'm going now with a wired hands-free with a boom, and the switch in the boom arm. MUCH BETTER. I hope someone from the design department at LG gets some feedback about this.

title: Mixing and matching with the older MM1000 isn't working...
text: Did not work for us. We're using two older MM1000 units. Removed one and replaced it with this newer MM1025, and it did not work. I know sometimes tech isn't straight forward as we'd like. There is probably some tinkering I need to do. But if the answer to the question, "Can we mix and match," is yes, then restarting my router/modem should be the most that I have to do on my end. But after restarting everything, this unit still did not work. Swapped it out with my older MM1000, and got my internet back. This is a 1-star until Motorola can help me solve the issue.

text:
According to AMFilm, This is a new product??

They now cover your front camera with glue and glass (see pic) which causes some cloudiness...duh.

They did away with the weight for the fingerprint sensor. I tried for 20 minutes to re-learn mine. After a bunch of "press harder" and "cover sensor" I gave up.

They went back to one glue application instead of two. The glue container was very slow to empty. I ended up with two bubbles in the lower part of the phone.

TL;DR - Bubbles, Fingerprint sensor issues, Front camera covered.

title: Not Currently Compatible with Tracfone or Magicjack
text:
Although I think the phone is great, it is incompatible with both Tracfone and Magicjack's WIFI MagicApp option, although both say they will support it sometime in the future. They refuse to provide estimates. Interestingly, Magicjack worked perfectly when first installed but failed later suggesting this is an administrative issue rather than a technical issue.

Otherwise it is a great value at a great price.

title: Great story..
text:
This game had a great story line. It was very interesting and easy to follow. It was medium in length and kept my interest through the whole thing. The graphics were gorgeous, I was quite pleased with it.

The down side is that the puzzles are almost impossible to figure out (most of them anyway), I had to cheat a lot to finish it. That is why it only gets a 3. If the puzzles made more sense it would have been a much better game.

title: Great small camera for it's price!
text:

Great for vacation, vlogging, etc. I am sure there are better ones on the market, but this is a nice level entry camera for its price. It's compact and easy to take with me wherever I need it. The only issue I have is that it didn't come with a battery charger. You have to charge the battery from the camera. Also, I like that it comes with a nice snug soft case to protect it plus sister batteries as well.

The controller never moves in my hands no matter how tight I grip it. Other sleeves have horizontal grooves that do not provide anywhere near the non-slip grip as the nubs. title: Very basic, great for drive in movies
text: I bought this to keep in my glove compartment to use at the drive in so I didn’t have to leave my engine running and get fumes in everyone’s face, or run down my battery. It’s a very basic radio, but if you’re patient, it works just fine. It is not digital, you do have to fiddle with the running knob and the antenna each time you move it. It didn’t work inside the car, only outside, so keep that in mind. It wouldn’t work well at the drive in if you plan on staying inside the car, but if you’re setting up chairs or tailgating it’s great!

title: Very difficult to use.
text:

Side note: It would be a difficult item to use traveling.

title: USB Type C Cable
text:
The description says these cables are gold plated. The ones I received are not, which is why I’ve taken off a star. However, in every other way, they’re great cables.

The braided nylon means the cable will hold up to a lot of use, even when I’m not careful with my cords. I like the gray color -- a small thing, but it’s a nice change from black or white. The cables seem sturdy and are reinforced at each end. I’ve used the cables for about a month with no signs of trouble.

title: 50$ bluetooth reciever, ten dollar speakers, 5 dollar headset.
text: The only working stealth 400 is a repaired stealth 400. These headsets break very easy. Less than a year of light duty and I had a broken mic button and two snapped off speakers. Repaired the speakers by fitting shaped steel under high strength electrical tape and now they are better than when new, they stay on my head better at least.

title: SO LOUD
