Perhaps on the East or West Coasts it might be more accurate, but for the country roads and small towns of Texas, Louisiana, Arkansas and Mississippi, this just didn't work well at all. Deleted it.

I will say that it works - there's no error messages from my laptop, no issues. However, it seems to say 'not charging' when it shows it being plugged in, and it holds the laptop at a steady charge while I'm using it. So it's not perfect, but as long as my pc is charged/has some charge when I use this cord and it doesn't cause any problems, I'm happy.

title: Serviceable

The plastic cover allows you display your own artwork (or use a template from the Wilson Jones website). Made of anti glare material, it lies flat without sagging or wrinkling. These covers have been an issue on other binders in this line but this one has no issues. The four interior pockets allow you to tuck loose papers. There is a generous five year guarantee. Overall, this is a binder that is satisfactory in all respects.

title: Tape a wet noodle to your phone

The only way this would work is if you super glued it on... Save yourself some money and get something that works so you don't drop your phone in the toilet.

I do however, wish it had some thing to grip the desktop as it does move around a little if I go text on my phone while it's on the stand. text:
This would get 5 stars if the direction of the plugs were flipped. The ground plug is on the inside rather than the outside. So if you have transformer style plugs, the face in and cover the opposite plug. If you use plug extensions, this won't be a problem. But they space the plugs to allow for transformers, so the direction of the plugs makes no sense.

The style is nice and it comes with USB charging ports, so you just need a cord to charge your phone or tablet. Also it has a nice long cord that lays down flat well. Less chance of tripping.

title: Great access, but glitchy.
text: We love the functionality of the Roku and use it often, especially for Netflix and Amazon Prime Direct. However, there is some kind of glitch in the system and we get an error message or it freezes about once a week. The fix is easy - just turn everything off and disconnect the HDMI cable for about 20 seconds and it resets itself, but it is inconvenient to do frequently. We have tried two different new HDMI cables with no change. If you google the HDCP unauthorized content error, you will see this is not an isolated issue.

title: Terrible design
text:
This isn't a gaming mouse. This is garbage.

1) Not mentioned in the advertisement is that this mouse uses fixed increments of 250 DPI.
Starting DPI is 500, then goes 750, 1000, 1250. Totally useless if you want to go 400 DPI, 800 DPI.

2) The buttons on the left hand side of the mouse are way too small. There is no way to distinguish by feel, which one you are pressing! Worse, the ridge of the left click panel runs directly up against it so you can get confused that you are pressing that as the button.

title: Only Works With Certain Types of Hair?
text: I really wanted this to work as advertised, but it just didn't. I have very fine hair, but I have a lot of it. Unfortunately, when I used in on clean, blow-dried hair, it tangled and left strange "fuzzy" ridges that were supposed to be curls, but just didn't look that way. I really think the issue is that when the outside barrel spins, it's so far away from the heating rod that the hair twists up and only the hair that is rolled on tightly gets any heat. It honestly just makes more sense to use a heating wand and wind your hair.

text: Fun game, bonus every hour. Gives extra bonus if you give a five star review so we can assume not all the reviews are accurate.

title: Stick with this since I bought the printer. Too ...
text: Stick with this since I bought the printer. Too expensive, especially because there are 7 cartridges. Quickly runs out and if you dont use it frequently enough it clogs. Then you have to run the cleaning program - which, incidentally uses up more of your ink

title: They came off of a few things easily, but others not so much
text: The description online is, "They adhere to almost any smooth surface including paper, cardboard, plastic, wood, glass and metal." I used them in a move for most of these items, and found that they were frequently not removable. They came off of a few things easily, but others not so much. I am now struggling to get the adhesive off and am quite disappointed that I trusted these "removable" labels. I would caution using these. If you decide to try them, I would place them on very lightly.

title: Good value. Arrived quickly.
text: Easy to use, appears to be durable.

title: Battery Drains Too Quickly
text:
Definitely needs some work. I made sure to completely drain the battery and fully charge a couple of times. But better doesn't last long. I would not be able to go a full day without charging (and that's without really using my phone). Would not recommend.

**Sellar contacted me via email and wanted me to change my rating to 5 stars and then they'll refund me. What a way to run your business!**

title: Cheap but useable
text:
So the product, like all in this price range, is made in china. The sound quality is okay for a 5 dollar set of earbuds, but definitely not a great everyday pair. It's more useful as a spare. The quick pair you can throw it in your pocket pair where you don't have to worry about it breaking. Don't expect the depth and detail you would get from a pricier set. Not much to say about them though, but if you need a really cheap set then get these.
DEFINITLY NOT AUDIOPHILE GRADE!! No one expects them to be obviously. If these break in a reasonable time, I will update my review.

title: Nice moisturizer but didn't really brighten up my skin much
text:
This lightenup cream comes in a fairly generous tube and runs you about $40. It has a few vitamins in it that help reduce fine lines but also should help brighten up the skin.

It has a fairly light fragrance that I do like. It is lightweight and absorbs quickly into the skin. I found it leaves no residue left behind on the skin which is great. I did not notice a significant brightening effect with this lotion though which was a bit disappointing in my eyes since that was the major claim of this lotion. It is nicely moisturizing though.

title: Great buy; can take it with me on my ...
text: Great buy;can take it with me on my travels.

title: Does the job
text: Pretty standard screen protector but have one bubble i could not get out.

title: Beautiful Case...Material Quality Could Be Better
text: This is a beautiful phone case, however the quality of the material could be improved upon.

title: 4 out of 5 stars because there is always room for improvement.
text: Product is good. Keeps everything neat and orderly. I have had it for just shy of a week and I have had no issues yet. My only concern is the location where you put the controllers; It's not a smooth transition plugging them in and removing them. I feel like the charging ports are fragile. As long as you take precaution they should last though. The additional accessories for the controllers is a nice touch. I was needing a replacement pad for one of the joy sticks so the fact that they were included was fantastic. 4 out of 5 stars because there is always room for improvement.

title: Easy, durable and worth every penny!
text: I've had mine now for well over a year and it's still in place and not a scratch or bend on it. I would highly recommend this product. I've dropped my phone more than I care to admit and still - it's on and looks great!

title: so nice to get this delivered to my door
text: so nice to get this delivered to my door, and not have to lugg it up to my apartment. plus the paper is good quality

title: DO NOT BUY.
text: The smell is horrible. I thought it was going to be thick for my extremely dry skin. Nope, it's thin. It's also not eligible for returns so I'm stuck with this gross smelling, basically useless product.

title: Bundle was more available…
text: This is our second PS5 purchased, and we opted for the bundle out of convenience. The console without the game was waitlisted while this bundle was available without a waitlist. We have yet to play the game, but I’m sure it will get played at some point. The bundle pricing saves you money on the price of the game if your interest in owning the game too. Overall, we love our PS5’s and think this was a great purchase. They quality of the system is incredible and well priced in my opinion.

text: Not too bad for the money. I do feel like I have to really press to get the color to go one though. I do like that there is a mix of pretty colors.

title: Beautiful band
text: The Rose Gold on this black leather strap matches my Apple Watch beautifully. I get a lot of comments about how pretty the band is. It is a very comfortable band as well. This is the second leather band I have purchased from this company. I am very pleased with this product and highly recommend it.

title: ok

title: Poor quality, cable immediately breaks
text: Just bought this have have only used it once, with very light use. Have not pulled, bent or tucked at the cable in any way, yet the cable frayed right at the base exposing the wires. Save your money and purchase a better product.

title: Disgusting smell that turns into nothing after two minutes
text: This smells nothing like suntan lotion I’ve tried pretty much everything out there this was the worst it smelled horrible and after two minutes it’s not like nothing! Yuck 🤢 yuck 🤮

title: Totally not as advertised.
text: apparently not as advertised. This is an NEW 3DS XL, not the titled OLD MODEL. The title advertises OLD MODEL, the description says NEW MODEL. What's up with that? It does not come with a charger as some have stated. Straight out disappointing.

title: Poor Design-Sent Back
text: I did not like this at all. I sent it back. Was not easy to get it level. Very narrow for using in bed - I felt like my legs had no room to stretch out to change position. Poor design.

title: Did not work for me!
text:
This Oribe Imperial Blowout Transformative Styling Spray is not really a spray it is a pump bottle cream. I was disappointed because my hair is just as flat as it was before I used it. I did as the instructions stated but it did not work for me.

The box comes with a 5-ounce pump bottle. There are instructions on the back of the bottle. The bottle does not match the title. It says Imperial Blowout Transformative Styling Creme.

I will not be recommending this Oribe Imperial Blowout Transformative Styling Spray to family and friends.

title: Recommend for Ages 40-50
text:
This sleep mask is really good. It's just a little late for me. The ingredients that work on my skin have changed with time, and I would have LOVED to have this mask when I was in my 40s.

It's got natural ingredients that will improve your circulation, and HLA to plump and moisturize. Even at my age, it soaks in great and makes me look less decrepit in the a.m.

Recommend.

title: Broke within two months
text: Mic broke within a couple of weeks. Within two months it stopped working completely. My son (8 yrs) is still disappointed, he got it with his report card money.

title: Worth the money
text: Worth the price. The colors are nice and they are easy to write with.

title: Gets the Job Done
text: Very adhesive, stays in place and does what it's supposed to do. I only hope that it comes off as easily as it goes on.

title: Nice Assortment of Brushes
text: I've started wearing eye makeup again. I figure that with the pandemic, I might as well focus on my eyes since they're visible while wearing my face mask! My previous eye shadow brushes were in need of replacing, so these arrived at the perfect time. This is a nice assortment of brushes. They are soft and hold the makeup adequately. I haven't noticed any shedding. I like having the variety of brushes with their specific purposes.

title: Works Very Well In Our Home Theater
text: I really like this Sony Amp. We have a dedicated home theater in our house, so this is amp is connected to a projector and 7.1 Klipsch speakers. I think this amp was easy to set up and operate, and to us it sounds very good. Considering the price and what it is doing for me, I think it is a 5 star amp. There may be some other audiophiles out there who are looking for a lot more in their amp, in which case this amp or review is not likely for you.

title: Fast charge
text: Excellent chargers that are nice and long. The one that comes with the Apple Watch is too short. I like having more than one charger, these are very handy to have around.

title: Simple but wrong colors
text: If colors hadn't been a bright neon in the pattern I would not have returned it.

title: Nice keyboard tray no tools required!
text: I have a curved IKEA corner desk and was worried that the tray wouldn't clamp properly with the curve, but it works well. I wish it were slightly longer, because I can't fit my mouse and keyboard together on the tray but since I use my mouse a lot more than the keyboard, I will still give it a good score because it meant I didn't have to buy a new desk! Feels very sturdy and made well.

title: Easy to assemble
text: A bit cheap. Easy to assemble. I guess it’s what you’d expect for the price. Not disappointed but also not impressed with the quality or look.

title: Five Stars
text: We will reorder as soon as possible

title: machines titans
text: Fun, timed game. So far they haven't required money. That is always a good thing. Addicting game.

title: Nothing like a wedding money card you have ever seen
text: If you are giving a gift of cash for a wedding, this is a very nice. It has a nice sentiment inside and has room for money. The front features a deep red imitation jewel and beneath that, the Chinese double happiness symbol accented with crimson foil. This card is elegant and so different from others that you have seen.

title: A Card A Mother Will Love
text: This is a pretty card that is sweet without being saccharin. The cover design is pretty with a rhinestone and a touch of glitter. It is definitely not overdone and the restraint is refreshing. The purple ribbon is pretty especially if you r mom likes purples. I thought it was a tad dark but certainly not a deal breaker. It is traditional with contemporary touches that make it less formal. It comes with a lavender colored envelope.

title: Great, inexpensive way to control one's hair and have ...
text:
Great, inexpensive way to control one's hair and have simple, inexpensive matching Hairbands.
Lets have more!!!! Sequined, braided, color combos,
Emojis pin-ones...etc., etc..
Simple, Classic and functional.

title: Disappointed
text: This is a very cute clutch and I will be keeping it. I’m disappointed that there is no cushioning to protect the switch if I were to use it without a case. It should just be sold a clutch. There’s so much space and nothing protects the actual switch lite.

text: The app works great! Clear picture, sound, etc. However, the selection of movies and tv shows are just terrible. I will stick with buying groceries and other products on amazon, not watching videos.

I have a pair of USB headphones that light up in the same style as this stand. Perfect, right? Well... no. I plugged the headphones into the Stand and somehow that’s not doable. Plug it into my powered hub? no prob.

Pity the usb hub on this isnt so hot otherwise its pretty neat.

The cover color is nice and professional looking; the stitching and the sturdy cover seems like it will keep the entire journal intact even with regular heavy use.

Some of the buttons feel rather mushy and you can’t really tell if you pressed it or not. In addition, the packaging was rather unprofessional and the software was kind of sketchy. I am going to try a different product; returning this during the current situation seems like more hassle than it's worth.

title: Will Not Work With All Sharpies
text: I had a combination of Sharpie Fine Point and Ultra Fine Sharpies. I tried numerous times to fit both into the holder and no go. Then I tried just the Fine Point... worked. Next, just the Ultra Fine Point... no go. It turns out it's all about the different ends of the two Sharpies models. Ultra have a flat end and the Fine have a tapered end which fits in the bottom recess of the holder.

title: It fit perfectly! But went air born after 24 days

Had to get a broom. It picked up all the dust from the floor. Had to toss it out. I have on the second screen hope this one stays on. Its been 6 months. The second screen popped off and fell on the carpet. I had to reorder another pack.

So I bought this to use on my Sony S3500 bluray to play a PAL dvd...because supposedly the product FAQs said this could convert a PAL to NTSC or at the very least play a PAL or something (I didn't think one had to be a tech wiz in order to play a supposedly "region free" DVD)

title: Handyman Special
text:
Great headset for those who can McGuyver their way out of any badly designed pigeonhole. You MUST reinforce the ear pad swivel joint before it breaks because it is guaranteed to do so unless you keep them unused in the china closet. If not them liberally glob some hot glue, bondo or bazooka joe around the broken part and marvel at your ingenuity.
This product is a perfect example of the junk that gets sold to unwitting buyers when companies place short term profit over product quality and customer satisfaction. They know they are bad but will not refund your money.

title: Pages aren't perforated
text:
So this book is adorable. The cover is removable so you can order replacements for it and continue to use it. My only complaint is that the pages are not perforated. So if you aren’t wanting to use it as a journal… but like me a grocery list… you can’t tear the pages out. Its bound by string so with every page you tear it loosens until it will fall apart.

Yoobi donates a product for every product you buy… so it’s a win win.

title: Glass screen protector
text: This protector was very easy to install, which is worth mentioning as some are not. I have a 6Splus and the cover is a bit short around the edges, which i really needed, as have some slight shattering on bottom corner, which did not get covered up. The protector did get a scratch on it quickly, however, think it happened in my purse from keys. I would not buy again, due to sizing.

title: Cheap and unreliable
text: The controller itself feels light and weak. You can hear rattling inside. Also, the R2/right trigger button is all screwed up now after only a short time using it. It registers but lost its spring and kind of sinks in when you press it now. And most fun of all, you never know when your PC/Steam will actually detect it. While playing a game, it will randomly not work and you will have to unplug and replug it for it to register, despite all of the drivers being current. Save yourself the headache and get something better than this cheap plastic trash.

title: Forces you to upgrade unnecessarily
text: I upgraded from earlier edition and I noticed a few minor improvements. I don't like that they make you upgrade every three years whether its necessary or not. Its just built into the program if you want to use online banking. Too tacky.

title: Sony Xperia XZ1 Compact Case
text:
I could not find a good tempered glass protector for my phone. I just decided to buy this case instead.
The case has been working really well for my Xperia XZ1 Compact. I like the dark grey color. Phone fits well.

title: A Nice Checkbook Register
text: I'm looking for a regular, three-column checkbook register and this is not it -- it does a nice, neat job of listing the transactions in chronological order, but not in the 3-column format that I am looking for -- however, the overall appearance and especially the function of this checkbook register is EXCELLENT if you are okay with the format.

title: Have thin hair this will work
text: The product is very thin and I ordered the 15 inch. Hair is good quality. If you have thin hair this will work for you.

title: Perfect Gift for Teacher!
text:
Well with the craziness that is Covid 19 I'm not sure you will have the chance to gift your teacher with this adorable Apple Note Dispenser, but if you do you will not be disappointed. It comes with coordinating post-it notes so you won't have to purchase those separately.

I took off one star because whatever is inside the apple itself to help weigh it down was somehow "leaking" out. I'm not sure if it just got too jostled in shipping but I don't even know where it was coming out of. It's not liquid just little beads.

title: Priced like the best but not the best
text: Work best with Apple products. ANC is improved. Adaptive transparency is interesting. The friction fit only design still falls out way too easily. The Pro2 case is easier to find; however, the whole location thing is a bit wonky. Still fundamentally flawed due to lack of graphic equalizer adjustments. Are they worth the cost to upgrade? No. Are they worth the cost compared to Bose or Sony buds. Objectively, no.

title: Too loose and not adjustable
text: Unfortunately this strap didn’t work for me. Too loose and doesn’t adjust. Cute pattern though so it’s disappointing.

title: Lipstick
text: This is a smooth, creamy lip color. It is very light and thin feeling on the lips. It has a soft color to it. It has absolutely zero staying power. Like, if you think about touching something with your lips it disappears. It doesn’t smear around, but definitely comes off on cups and such. Reapplication throughout the day is necessary. But the feel of it is really nice. It looks nice.

title: A good home budget planner
text: I bought this budget book to help my husband and I actually track where are money was going. Instead of just writing down amounts in a category this book gives you both a category and a day to write the actual amounts in. So if we eat out say several times a month we write the amounts in the category and then with the corresponding day. It gives me a quick glance ability to see how often we are actually eating out, or how often we buy gas, etc.

title: Nice 2-hole wahl product
text: I am a barber of 10 years and this is my best clipper yet. Although I have small hands it is a little heavier than others I have used, it is a great product and I am very pleased. There is one problem I have found, after a couple years of usage, it skips after I turn it on, but only on occasion, like every few months. I am not sure if it is from the outlet or the product wiring but it's happened in 2 different shops. Also, as with any clipper, they need to be oiled regularly.

title: Both copies produced spurious button presses
text:
It works OK but for produces occasional wrong button presses. I push down, it goes left/right. Half of my Tetris games were ruined by one bad press.

This might mean that the button is constructed in a way that allows an imprecise or uncentered button press to register wrong. It's probably a combination of my fingers, and the button design.

I got a more expensive Buffalo controller for Christmas and now every button press is exactly what I intended. Not one bad press.

If you can, try this before you buy it, but if you have the money, I'd suggest the Buffalo.

text:
I've purchased several of these over the past 6 months, and so far, all of them are still working just fine. The oldest is 6 months old and shows no signs of wear; no failures yet, either. It's the one that travels with me on my weekly 2-day rehearsal runs to Seattle (95 miles away). The other two stay plugged in to my chargers here at the house. One is used daily, and is holding up just fine.

I'd read some of the other reviews that said these don't hold up well, but that hasn't been my experience. So far, so good ...

title: Cheap, with pretty bad sound
text: Classic “you get what you pay for” moment here. The sound quality is pretty awful. Yes, it works fine for phone calls, and you can hear and be heard ok (not great, but ok), and pairing is easy. That’s about it. You can’t listen to music or anything else without being quickly annoyed at the internal rattle from the abysmal speaker system. It’s vastly worse than using your speakerphone. Also, the clip is so narrow that it didn’t work for anywhere in my car. Literally no place. So best case scenario, it can sit in my lap or cup holder, both of which are bad choices. Nope. Not great.

title: Too Easy - Very Ad Aggressive

But this one relies too much on 3-letter words: eat, ate, tea and are, ear, era, etc. It often ignores other words that could be made from the main 5 or 6 letters. And it inevitably chooses common words instead of secondary - but more interesting - words. Also, a bit stingy on extra words. At around level 230, the words become more interesting. But, it's a long slog to get there. About ads: There is an ad between every, single level of the game. I just deleted the whole thing.

title: the best weather resource
text: Here in Texas we say ' if you don't like the weather, just wait a day --it will change', and it does. From extreme drought to widespread flooding overnight is not unusual. We need to know for our crops, livestock,and businesses to be accurately informed. Weather bug gives you up to the minute, accurate, professional, and realistic information. I like the visuals real well, e.g. dopplar radar, precipitation, wind dpeed, etc. You can get what you need in one place. I highly recommend this app if you need the right information.

The current price of $8 is reasonable for this brush.

title: Good quality and great value. Will purchase again.
text: I used to be a stickler for Canon inks for my Canon printer, but when you can get a collection like this for 1/3 of what you'd pay to refill the printer once with Canon's ink cartridges, it's worth trying the cheaper brand. I have been very happy with these inks. I have adhered to the 3-minute wait before taking the cap off and have not had a problem with leakage. In fact, these cartridges light up in my Canon Pixma 882 even brighter than one of the other brands I'd tried. My printer doesn't use the grey cartridge but the others seem to work fine. I will definitely reorder again.

This ultra fine point is about the width of a ball point pen, maybe just slightly wider, but OK for my use. I ordered the assortment of colors for $8.51with free Shipping for Prime Members and this is a fairly good price per pen also. I really like them for a lot of markings color options.

The sound of the headset is fine, but it doesn't make up for the failure rate, the shoddy USB cable (that showed signs of wear very quickly), and the general experience. The surround sound didn't do much for me and I usually didn't notice when I accidentally would toggle it off.

title: Bought refiurbished, did not work, tech support worthless

I bought two of these refurnished expecting them to be reset to factory settings. They were not. As a result, I could not get into them. Called tech support, and they were never able to give me any useful suggestions. In the end, they turned into landfill because my time to return them expired.

Never buying refurbished gear from this company again.

title: presses the headset button!

Shae butter is a plant product that produces a very rich and creamy lotion. Feels great to use and smells nice, too. This set is a great introduction to these products. The soap and lotion are also available separately in larger sizes.

title: It Helps!

This nail product is helping to solve the problem. I've noticed that I don't have to trim it as often. It seems to be fortifying the weak spot.

title: Nice Basic Binders
text: These binders are basic but ideal for office or home use. The O rings function and the maximum capacity is 175 pages. The cover and spine are customizable and the inside features the standard pockets. It is designed to lie flat when opened. I think they are ideal for storage but probably not sturdy enough to withstand the rigors of daily use. Overall, these are ice basic binders.

title: Not what I expected.
text: From the reviews I expected this to be wonderful. - Not so much. I normally love Josie's products but this one fell short for me. It was tacky and sticky. Rather than moisturizing, it actually seemed to dry my lips. It was too late to return by the time I made up my mind about the product, and I actually threw it in the trash.

title: Pretty & Useless
text: Very pretty! That's about the only good thing. It is indeed form fitting. It's so form fitting that there's nothing to hold onto when you're trying to read. Reading one-handed is impossible with this case. The screen falls away and faces opposite direction, and you're left holding a floppy mess as there's no firm material between your hand and the Fire screen to hold it in place. Pretty, form fitting, and utterly useless functionally. Perhaps they're not building the Fire and accessories for reading anymore. That's a shame.

title: If You Need It....
text: Windows comes with this built-in. Linux has free utilities. But if you need advanced features like the ability to create password protected zip files, then this is a great option. I first used Winzip back in the early 1990s, and today it is still the best commercially available software for the job. I will note that if you are a power user, there is free software that does the job out there.

title: Lots of latency and dead spots.

title: Excellent for Movies, but ...

However, it is less than amazing for music, as it is deficient in the tweeter category, so your treble gets lost.
Movie buffs and gamers can rejoice - music fans won't be ecstatically happy.

title: Difficult

I found the thing with holding the arrows extremely difficult, I am a seasoned DDR player and I failed some normally easy songs because of the stupid hold steps. A lot of people might like this game but it frustrated me to no end. A good point is that they did not change this one from the Japanese version. Even though it is a hard game, I still say Rock On DDR!!!

title: Solid performer
text:
The thing that I like best about this Kbear Wired Earbuds is that the cord does not tangle. Given how annoyed I get waiting for wireless earphones to pair (or when the pair fails), I'm starting to prefer wired again, but the cord tangle is annoying too so this product's cord being non-tangle is a win to me.

The sound quality is okay. I'm good with it. It does come with different sizes of earbuds as per the standard. It has a friendly price point as well.

Overall, I give it a 4-star as a solid performer.

title: Boring.
text: Didn't hold my interest to get past the first challenge

title: Works great. Now if I just had a device to ...

title: Four Stars
text: A good app to have for the amazon fire. Spent a decent amount of time here.

title: Fun and easy.
text: Fun game. Something my young son and I can do together.

title: Supershieldz Screen Protector
text: They are not very good. They leave an oily bubble in the middle of the screen, maybe it will disappear. The also left glue lines along the sides. The display is not impressive and actually dulls the image. The only positive is the touch responsiveness. Try other brands.

title: Light Conditioning, Good Scent
text: We were not overwhelmed by this conditioner. It is noticeably harder to come through our hair after using it so it is not a strong detangler and it even left my partner wondering if she had forgotten to use a conditioner. Her hair looked fine after using it, but nothing obviously better. I did like the scent when I used it, but not the conditioner for us. I did like this companies curl defining shampoo much better than this conditioner.

title: Flavor is pretty strong
text: The flavor is pretty strong for me. I use it to clean my teeth, I don’t think it whitens my teeth though

title: Same as Netflix
text: The programs I like on Hulu Plus were pretty much the same as on Netflix. Since I already have a Netflix account, didn't see the reason to join Hulu Plus

title: Great For Art From Your Kids
text: My two little girls are always bringing me pictures, and as much as I love the images I don't have the wall space to frame them all for permanent display. This is a nice folder that provides plenty of pockets so I can protect and organize their artwork. It has 24, 8X10" plastic sleeves and a durable cover. The sleeves are affixed to the spine of the folder and do not come loose like what sometimes happens with a three ring binder. This is a nice folder that would also have a variety of applications at school or the workplace.

title: Not as strong or affordable as another eye massager
text: I have a 3 piece steel eye massager set that includes this piece. That set is $18.99 plus an extra 10% off. This set is $16.99 plus an extra 10% off. The other thing this magnet isn't as strong. It mostly holds the ball, but it can drop out unexpectedly. I was holding it still in one hand when it suddenly just fell out. It is nicely boxed with a velvet storage bag and an extra ball - for when the first ball drops out and disappears, haha! These are nice, and it's great for hygiene that you can remove the ball, but I wouldn't recommend this particular one.

title: Too Expensive To Play For Long
text: Like most of these games they want money to play. Hard to win any if you never get a bingo.

title: Took too long to straighten
text: I didn’t love the straightener. It said it was good for short hair. I have short hair but I think this straightener would be better for pixie styled cuts. It just didn’t seem to get hot enough for my hair and took forever to get through all my hair. I will say it didn’t pull and was great for my postpartum hair growth.

title: Good tape
text: Good for little jobs - it’s tape, not glue. It works but it’s thin and flimsy

title: Great price, but beware
text: Killed my motherboard “screen inverter IC” after install, after much reading, the knock off screens don’t plug in well to the motherboard side well and cause shorting, common issue with this model. Phone still appears to function but can’t see anything due to backlight inverter circuit dead. Only fix now is to replace the motherboard.

title: Terrible

title: Arrived damaged.
text: Paper package arrived damaged. The shipping box was fine. Nothing else in the box was damaged. It was like the glue holding the package together just gave up the ghost. It looks like all the paper is there and only a few sheets are actually damaged, so it is not worth returning, However, I probably won't buy this again. The paper itself appears to be decent paper and the only reason I'm giving this 2 stars.

title: Pops on easily. Nice and thin
text: Pops on easily. Nice and thin, barely adding any weight or thickness, but it feels more secure. Wish I'd had it before I dropped my laptop and broke the screen. At least is protected now after the repair.

title: Does a great job, makes all the difference
text: Until I actually used a light ring with my iphone I didn't realize just what a difference it made. This holds the phone steady, provides variable light source that comes from all around. The only down size (and I marked off a star for it) is the tripod feels flimsy and I ended up getting a better tripod for this light.

Not happy.

title: Leaving black circles on iPad Pro screen
text:
Leaves black marks on my iPad screen. These are cheap but I've never seen this before. I'm really upset that these styluses left little black circle marks all over my new iPad Pro screen. Luckily, it seems to wipe off but I don't know what it is that's coming off the tips of these things.
Do not buy them . Not worth the savings and you might end up ruining a very expensive device!

title: Fun Then Pay

Bummer, can't fault the capitalism necessary to make money off of this product, but it had appeared to be a free game from the beginning. My fault, though the puzzles were pretty innovative.

title: Useful!
text:
If you use your headset, it's going to get dirty and sweaty etc. I used the pen a couple of times on the lens, I think it worked okay, but I usually use the cleaning cloth on the lens. I really don't want to scratch them. The lens cover is great.

Nobody wants their Quest 2 to fail because of sunlight hitting the lenses. The brush can also be used on the outer sensors. Keeping your Oculus clean from dust, sweat, and anything else goes a long way to help keep it working well.

title: Expensive but does keep skin moisturized
text: I have been using this oil for over two weeks now on a daily basis. Jury is still out on the pure benefits. On the plus side there are vitamins in this oil (but no retinol) so it is good for my skin. Goes on a little oily, takes a while to absorb. My skin does feel softer since using, haven't really seen a significant difference in elasticity or in the fine lines (crow's feet) around my eyes. On the negative side, at over $40 I don't think this is worth it. I have some really great facial serums at 30% of the price that, in my opinion, work much better.

title: Immediately gave me acne
text: I purchased this oil for my gua sha facial routine, and after using it only a few times it clogged my pores so much. It caused me to have little bumps when I haven’t gotten those for months. I’ll stick with my other facial oil and I’m returning this. It smells good but it’s not working for me.

title: Loads of fun! Just one gripe...
text:
This works great, is a lot of fun, and the miniature unit itself is cute! All the games we've tried look and sound great on our 65" LCD TV.
Only gripe is that the cable on the one included controller is ridiculously short. Like, three feet long. If you want to play 2 player you'll need another controller anyway, so look for controllers with a longer cable and just buy two. For ~$200 the unit should come with 2 usable controllers in the box.
Doesn't come with a power supply either but like lots of modern TVs ours has a powered USB-A in back, so no problem for us.

title: Instant Game Bundle Makes No Sense
text:
A second hand Vita costs less than $200 and a year of PS Plus is $50. An 8GB Memory Card is about $30.
So $180 Vita+$50 year of Plus+$30 8GB=$260.
You can save $40 and get double the storage space for games if you buy the parts of this bundle separately. Sony is pricing the Vita at above market value, and their bundles are incoherent.

title: Cajun

I am now completely satisfied with the wireless flash drive and encouraged by the promptness of Sandisk to resolve my issue. Great device and supported extremely well. Bravo Zulu!!!!

title: The Great Rebooting Router Mystery Tour
text:
This is an inexpensive, easy to setup router / firewall combo.

You'll be fine if you do no online gaming or anything requiring consistent connectivity.

There are ongoing, unresolved issues with DLink routers and how they handle a PADT packet from your ISP's equipment.

This router handles the command by not only disconnecting, but by also REBOOTING itself. This leads to a 15-30 second disconnect condition while the device reboots.

I'm sorry to say I really can't recommend this item (it's rebooted 3 times while writing this!)

title: Flimsy and small. This belongs in a dollar store.

title: Installation frame is BRILLIANT, but no front camera cut out
text:
The frame that comes with this allows you to install it totally centered with no effort. This is a big deal because with iPhones, the area right around the touch ID pad always gets air bubbles if the screen protector isn't dead center.

HOWEVER there is no cut out around the front camera, and the seller says the fogginess I'm seeing must be from dust, even though I've tried all 3 screen protectors now to make sure. I'll be buying different ones with cutouts.

title: Has limitations
text: The size of this remote is both a pro and a con. I love the built it light that is activated when the remote is bumped or moved... but it's easy to lose and the Infrared controls suck on the whole XBox One system if you are trying to control other devices. Even with IR repeaters / transmitters.... getting volume controls to work on the tv or soundbar is a real challenge.

title: There's a Learning Curve
text: There's definitely a learning curve to using these adhesive laminating sheets. I recommend doing a trial run before doing the real thing. My daughter had a few things she wanted to laminate. Her items were on cardstock which I think is easier to work with than regular paper because it's less likely to wrinkle during the process. She has been pleased with the results and is now looking for other things that would benefit from lamination.

title: Great idea, but not so great results
text: I purchased this to use in the gym with my iPod shuffle to avoid using wired earbuds. In the sense that it eliminated the wires, it was a success, but in terms of musical fidelity and blocking out extraneous sound, it was a failure. Back to my earbuds.
